note: Curated per-document dates and one-sentence descriptions for the F-1986-04261 declassified PDF release. Source of truth consumed by fetch_foia_pdfs.py (manifest enrichment) and build_core.py (timeline). Dates derived from cable date-time groups, telegram serials, explicit in-text dates, and conservative event-based inference; month-only and undated documents carry an empty `date`. Confidence: exact | partial | uncertain | none.

foia-C09000001.description: White House Executive Secretariat routing/action slip for a memorandum to the President concerning Reykjavik materials.

foia-C09000002.description: State Department memorandum on the release of Soviet U.N. employee Gennadiy Zakharov into Soviet custody (the Daniloff affair) and the fate of the human-rights monitoring group.

foia-C09000003.description: Bureau of Public Affairs 'Current Policy' compilation excerpting key U.S. statements on arms control, U.S.-Soviet relations and the Reykjavik meeting.

foia-C09000004.description: Office of the Secretary routing/action slip (recommendation-and-reply handling).

foia-C09000005.description: Embassy Reykjavik cable (86REYKJA02091) reporting the second Soviet pre-summit press conference, briefed by Nikolay Shishlin and Yevgeniy Primakov on regional conflicts and the world economy.

foia-C09000006.description: State Department routing/action slip with FOIA exemption-review markings.

foia-C09000007.description: Transcript of remarks at the Reykjavik press center reflecting on the two days of Reagan-Gorbachev meetings.

foia-C09000008.description: President Reagan's departure remarks at the Reykjavik/Keflavik airport thanking embassy staff after the two days of meetings with Gorbachev.

foia-C09000009.description: White House press-office news summary of weekend media coverage of the Reykjavik meeting.

foia-C09000010.description: Embassy Reykjavik cable on the leaders' arrival and press-filing-center arrangements as the meetings opened.

foia-C09000011.description: Embassy Reykjavik cable summarizing Icelandic media coverage on the eve of the Reagan-Gorbachev pre-summit (Oct 11 papers and state radio/TV).

foia-C09000012.description: Routing/action slip for a memorandum to the President.

foia-C09000013.description: Heavily degraded document concerning U.S.-Soviet discussions (source text largely illegible).

foia-C09000014.description: Embassy Reykjavik cable analyzing the propaganda themes in General Secretary Gorbachev's post-summit press conference.

foia-C09000015.description: Embassy Reykjavik cable (No. 10 in the media series) summarizing Icelandic coverage of Reagan-Gorbachev based on the Oct 12-13 editions.

foia-C09000016.description: State cable summarizing highlights of the Sunday, Oct 12 evening network television news coverage of the summit's inconclusive end.

foia-C09000017.description: USNATO cable (86USNATO 05508) transmitting the transcript of Secretary Shultz's on-the-record press conference at NATO headquarters on October 13.

foia-C09000018.description: Executive Secretariat routing/action slip for a memorandum to the President.

foia-C09000019.description: Embassy Moscow cable (86MOSCOW017709) on Soviet media treatment of Reykjavik, citing Western and American commentary.

foia-C09000020.description: State cable (86STATE 322566) relaying the transcript of Shultz's NATO press conference to Ambassador Price in London.

foia-C09000021.description: Information memorandum to the Secretary on Gorbachev's hour-long televised appearance calling the Iceland meeting an important new situation.

foia-C09000022.description: Office of the Secretary routing/action slip.

foia-C09000023.description: Executive Secretariat action slip for an outgoing State cable and White House correspondence.

foia-C09000024.description: Executive Secretariat memorandum (Nicholas Platt to VADM Poindexter) transmitting draft Reykjavik op-ed pieces for Shultz and Ridgway.

foia-C09000025.description: Draft op-ed 'The Reykjavik Meeting' assessing the extraordinary but ultimately disappointing outcome of the Reagan-Gorbachev talks.

foia-C09000026.description: Draft op-ed on the Reykjavik meeting (Ridgway version) stressing progress on arms-control issues despite no final agreement.

foia-C09000027.description: Draft op-ed on the Reykjavik meeting reviewing the four negotiating areas (human rights, regional conflicts, bilateral matters, arms control).

foia-C09000028.description: Routing/action slip for a memorandum to the President.

foia-C09000029.description: Office of the Secretary routing/action slip.

foia-C09000030.description: Executive Secretariat control/action slip for an outgoing State cable.

foia-C09000031.description: Embassy Bucharest cable (86BUCHAR07545) on Foreign Minister Shevardnadze's October 16 press conference after Warsaw Pact meetings, taking a tough public line on Reykjavik.

foia-C09000032.description: Embassy Helsinki cable (86HELSINKI05058) reporting Soviet Ambassador Semyonov's October 15 briefing of Finnish President Koivisto on the summit.

foia-C09000033.description: Embassy Rome cable on a special Soviet Moscow envoy's meeting with Italian Prime Minister Craxi about arms-control negotiating positions after Reykjavik.

foia-C09000034.description: USNATO cable (86USNATO 05622) transmitting a correction to a prior message on the two-stage elimination of strategic offensive weapons discussed at Reykjavik.

foia-C09000035.description: State cable circulating unclassified talking points on the Reykjavik meetings to Nordic country desks for use with local contacts.

foia-C09000036.description: State cable reporting a Washington briefing by DAS Simons for NATO, Korean, Japanese and Australian embassies on the Reykjavik meeting.

foia-C09000037.description: Embassy Moscow cable on the October 21 press conference where Deputy Foreign Minister Bessmertnykh and negotiator Karpov criticized the U.S. and cited alleged presidential quotes from Reykjavik.

foia-C09000038.description: Embassy Moscow cable (DTG 281605Z OCT 86) supplying Russian-language transliterations of Bessmertnykh's alleged quotes of the President at Reykjavik.

foia-C09000039.description: Executive Secretariat routing/action slip for a memorandum to the President.

foia-C09000040.description: Transcript of National Security Adviser John Poindexter's on-the-record briefing at Andrews Air Force Base on the Reykjavik meeting.

foia-C09000041.description: Routing/action slip for a memorandum to the President.

foia-C09000042.description: Bureau of Public Affairs 'Current Policy' compilation excerpting key U.S.-Soviet statements, including Shultz's Los Angeles World Affairs Council address.

foia-C09000043.description: Action memorandum with talking points and scenarios for the Secretary's pre-Reykjavik congressional leadership briefing (October 7 bipartisan breakfast).

foia-C09000044.description: Memorandum on the Secretary's October 8 breakfast with Senator Lugar to brief him on the upcoming meeting with Gorbachev.

foia-C09000045.description: State cable reporting Secretary Shultz's meeting with Morris Abram and leaders of major Jewish organizations on Soviet human rights ahead of Reykjavik.

foia-C09000046.description: NIACT cable to all NATO capitals conveying the President's September 30 announcement that he and Gorbachev would meet October 11-12 in Reykjavik.

foia-C09000047.description: State cable updating allies on Soviet criticisms voiced at the October 7 defense-and-space negotiating plenary in Geneva.

foia-C09000048.description: USNATO cable (86USNATO 05509) reporting DAS Tom Simons' October 13 Brussels briefing of Japanese MOFA official Watanabe on the Reykjavik results.

foia-C09000049.description: Embassy Moscow cable analyzing Gorbachev's October 14 televised speech laying out the Soviet version of the Reykjavik meeting.

foia-C09000050.description: State cable directing posts to brief host governments on Secretary Shultz's October 13 NAC consultations on Reykjavik.

foia-C09000051.description: Embassy Tokyo cable (86TOKYO) on Ambassador Rowny's one-hour October 15 meeting with Foreign Minister Kuranari briefing on Reykjavik.

foia-C09000052.description: Embassy Canberra cable on Ambassador Adelman's meetings with the Australian Prime Minister and senior officials reassuring them on post-Reykjavik arms control.

foia-C09000053.description: Embassy Seoul cable reporting the briefing of South Korean President Chun on the Reykjavik meeting.

foia-C09000054.description: State cable reporting Assistant Secretary Ridgway's October 15 briefing of Warsaw Pact ambassadors and charges on Reykjavik.

foia-C09000055.description: Embassy Beijing cable (86BEIJIN026629, DTG 180245Z OCT 86) on Ambassador Rowny's October 16 post-Reykjavik consultations with Chinese Vice Foreign Minister Zhu Qizhen.

foia-C09000056.description: Embassy Rome cable reporting Secretary Weinberger's October 18 meeting with Italian Defense Minister Spadolini on Reykjavik and U.S. arms-control proposals.

foia-C09000057.description: Embassy Moscow cable (86MOSCOW018493) on the Soviet public-media campaign on Reykjavik, in full swing two weeks after the summit.

foia-C09000058.description: Document whose scanned image yielded no extractable text (blank or illegible page).

foia-C09000059.description: Draft congressional letter urging bipartisan consensus on nuclear-testing policy and the Defense Authorization Act ahead of the Reykjavik meeting.

foia-C09000060.description: Letter thanking Icelandic hosts for briefings and logistics and casting Reykjavik as a working meeting rather than a summit.

foia-C09000061.description: Information memorandum (Richard Solomon to the Secretary) on links between Soviet human-rights behavior and economic development for the Reykjavik pre-summit.

foia-C09000062.description: State NIACT cable (DTG 301727Z SEP 86) to NATO capitals conveying the September 30 announcement that Reagan and Gorbachev would meet October 11-12 in Reykjavik.

foia-C09000063.description: USNATO cable (86USNATO 05509) reporting DAS Simons' October 13 Brussels briefing of Japanese MOFA official Watanabe on the Reykjavik results.

foia-C09000064.description: Senate Republican Policy Committee 'End of Session' talking points, including a section on the Iceland (Reykjavik) summit.

foia-C09000065.description: Table of contents and annotated agenda for the President's Reykjavik visit briefing book, covering the October 9-12 schedule.

foia-C09000066.description: State cable (86STATE 311155, DTG 022138Z OCT 86) transmitting an unofficial translation of a Soviet speaking note to Europeans on the announced Reykjavik meeting.

foia-C09000067.description: Information memorandum (Richard Solomon to the Secretary) analyzing links between Soviet human rights and economic development for Reykjavik pre-summit preparations.

foia-C09000068.description: Information memorandum (Richard Solomon) giving the Secretary talking points on human-rights issues for use at the Reykjavik meeting.

foia-C09000069.description: Embassy Addis Ababa cable (86ADDIS 05204, with State guidance) on the regional-conflict dimension of Reykjavik, focused on Ethiopia and the Horn of Africa.

foia-C09000070.description: Embassy Moscow cable (86MOSCOW017836) commenting on Gorbachev's October 14 televised speech blaming SDI for preventing a breakthrough at Reykjavik.

foia-C09000071.description: State cable on Secretary Shultz's October 13 NAC consultations, tasking posts with allied briefings on Reykjavik.

foia-C09000072.description: Embassy Beijing cable (section 01 of 86BEIJIN026629, DTG 180245Z OCT 86) on Ambassador Rowny's October 16 post-Reykjavik consultations with Chinese VFM Zhu Qizhen.

foia-C09000073.description: Embassy Moscow cable (86MOSCOW018493, DTG 241615Z OCT 86) on the Soviet public line two weeks after Reykjavik.
